FN02	FN	74	The altitudes $\overline{AD}$ and $\overline{BE}$ intersect at the orthocenter $H$ of $\triangle ABC$.

In $\triangle ABC$, $\angle BAC = 54^\circ$ and $\angle ABC = 52^\circ$, so $\angle ACB = 180^\circ - 54^\circ - 52^\circ = 74^\circ$.

In $\triangle ABH$, $\angle BAH = 54^\circ$ and $\angle ABH = 52^\circ$, so $\angle AHB = 180^\circ - 54^\circ - 52^\circ = 74^\circ$.

FN03	FN	76	Let $ x = 0.\overline{1331} $.

Then $ 10000x = 1331.\overline{1331} $.

Subtract: $ 10000x - x = 1331.\overline{1331} - 0.\overline{1331} $, so $ 9999x = 1331 $.

Thus, $ x = \frac{1331}{9999} $.

Simplify $ \frac{1331}{9999} $. Note that $ 1331 = 11^3 $ and $ 9999 = 3^2 \cdot 11^3 $, so $ \gcd(1331, 9999) = 11^3 = 1331 $.

Hence, $ \frac{1331 \div 1331}{9999 \div 1331} = \frac{1}{7 + 4/9} = \frac{1}{\frac{67}{9}} = \frac{9}{67} $.

Since $ \gcd(9, 67) = 1 $, $ a = 9 $, $ b = 67 $, and $ a + b = 76 $.

#### 76
